chunks.text:
How it works

OpenDetail follows a small two-part flow: build once from your Markdown and MDX, then answer questions from the resulting local index.

The design stays simple on purpose so it is easy to inspect and easy to reason about.
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Build time

At build time, OpenDetail:

reads the files matched by opendetail.tomloptionally maps local media files to public URLsparses Markdown and MDXsplits content into heading-based chunkswrites a local artifact to .opendetail/index.json

The output is file-based and local. There is no required vector database, sync service, or external search system in the default path.
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Request time

At request time, OpenDetail:

loads the local index into memorysearches it with lexical matchingselects the most relevant chunkssends only those chunks to the OpenAI Responses APIoptionally adds configured remote retrieval toolsreturns an answer with citations
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Grounding behavior

The runtime gives the model strict instructions:

answer from the provided sources and tool resultscite grounded factual statementsdo not fill missing gaps from general knowledgesay clearly when the topic is not documented

That is what makes OpenDetail a grounded docs assistant instead of a general chat layer.
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Images and sources

If matched chunks contain supported image references, OpenDetail returns grounded image metadata alongside the text answer. Each image lists the source IDs it came from so UI can connect visuals back to citations.

The runtime returns at most 3 images per answer or stream.
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Why the design stays narrow

This design keeps the product small, inspectable, and honest:

your content stays the source of truththe local index stays close to the appthe request contract stays easy to implementthe failure mode stays clear when docs are missing
chunks.title: How it works

chunks.text:
Visual references

opendetail can carry image references from your docs into the answer payload. That lets a UI show a visual next to the response instead of only rendering text and citations.

Thread shell reference
chunks.title: Visual references

chunks.text:
What the first visual demonstrates

The shell reference shows a narrow reading column with a fixed composer anchored near the bottom of the viewport. It is useful when a product wants the input to stay reachable while the answer stream grows above it.

Streaming response reference
chunks.title: Visual references

chunks.text:
What the second visual demonstrates

The streaming response reference emphasizes the assistant answer card, source metadata, and the way an image can arrive before the full body copy has finished streaming. That makes it a good test case for the live UI surface in the website app.
chunks.title: Visual references

chunks.text: FAQ
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: Does OpenDetail ship a chat UI?
chunks.text:
Does OpenDetail ship a chat UI?

No. OpenDetail is the engine and integration layer. The current package focuses on the build step, local index, runtime, and thin integration helpers.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
Is it built on OpenAI?

Yes. OpenDetail uses the OpenAI Responses API underneath.
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: Do I need a vector database?
chunks.text:
Do I need a vector database?

No. The default path builds a local JSON index and searches it locally.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
What content gets indexed?

Whatever matches the include globs in opendetail.toml, minus anything in exclude.
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: Does MDX still work if the file uses components?
chunks.text:
Does MDX still work if the file uses components?

Yes, but the important part is the text and headings. Retrieval is about the content, not the rendered component tree.
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: Can it answer from outside my docs?
chunks.text:
Can it answer from outside my docs?

Not by default. The local path is grounded in matched docs only. If you configure remote_resources, it can also cite those remote tool results.
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: What happens when the docs are not enough?
chunks.text:
What happens when the docs are not enough?

It answers plainly that the topic is not documented yet or cannot be confirmed from the configured docs.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
Where does the data live?

The built index lives in your app at .opendetail/index.json.
chunks.title: FAQ

headings: Is the hosted backend available yet?
chunks.text:
Is the hosted backend available yet?

No. The hosted integration is documented and scaffolded in this phase, but the OpenDetail-managed backend is not shipped yet.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
What frameworks are supported today?

Today the package ships a core Node runtime and a thin Next.js adapter through opendetail/next.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
Does it replace docs?

No. OpenDetail works because the docs already exist. It makes that material easier to ask, retrieve, and cite inside an app.
chunks.title: FAQ

chunks.text:
Hosted

hosted is intentionally small in this phase.

It documents and scaffolds the client-side transport shape, but the OpenDetail-managed proxy backend is not shipped yet.
chunks.title: Hosted

chunks.text:
What changes in hosted mode

opendetail setup --integration hosted does not generate a routethe request shape stays { question: string }the stream events stay meta, sources, images, delta, done, erroryour app reads a hosted endpoint from environment and passes it into the client or hook
chunks.title: Hosted

chunks.text:
Scaffold it

bunx opendetail setup --integration hosted

That writes opendetail.toml and builds .opendetail/index.json, then skips route scaffolding on purpose.
chunks.title: Hosted

chunks.text:
Environment shape

The hosted path expects your app to read:

OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINToptionally OPENDETAIL_AUTH_TOKEN

In browser-facing apps, the registry examples use NEXT_PUBLIC_OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT and NEXT_PUBLIC_OPENDETAIL_AUTH_TOKEN because the app is reading those values directly.
chunks.title: Hosted
Hosted

headings: What hosted does not do yet
chunks.text:
What hosted does not do yet

it does not ship the OpenDetail-managed backend itselfit does not remove the need to build your local indexit does not change the request or stream contract

The point of the hosted mode today is to make the future transport shape explicit without forcing a later contract change.
chunks.title: Hosted

chunks.text:
Self-hosted

self-hosted is the default, fully implemented path today.

Use it when you want your own app route, your own runtime, and your own OPENAI_API_KEY.
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
What you manage

the Next.js routethe runtime environmentthe OpenAI API keyrebuilding the local docs index when content changes
chunks.title: Self-hosted
Self-hosted

chunks.text:
Runtime requirements

Node.js 18 or newera Next.js Route Handler running on the Node.js runtimeOPENAI_API_KEY set in the runtime environmenta built local index at .opendetail/index.json
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
Request contract

The request body stays intentionally small:

{ "question": "How do I configure base_path?" }

Requests that do not match that shape return a JSON error response.
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
Stream contract

The route returns application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8.

The stream events are:

meta: configured model metadatasources: the matched local sources, plus remote citations when enabledimages: grounded image references from the matched docsdelta: streamed text chunksdone: the final accumulated texterror: a public error payload safe to show in UI
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
Initialization behavior

The Next.js adapter lazily creates and caches the assistant instance. If setup fails before streaming starts, the adapter returns a JSON error response instead of opening a broken stream.

In production, initialization errors are cached so repeated requests do not retry the same broken startup work on every request.
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
When to choose self-hosted

Choose this mode when:

your docs are privateyou want the route in your own appyou want full control over auth, runtime, and deploymentyou want the current end-to-end path, not the future hosted path
chunks.title: Self-hosted

chunks.text:
Overview

opendetail turns your Markdown and MDX docs into a small, grounded assistant for your app.

It sits on top of the OpenAI Responses API, but the value is not a new agent runtime. The value is a smaller setup path, grounded retrieval from your own docs, and a cleaner integration surface than wiring that stack by hand.
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
What OpenDetail is for

OpenDetail fits best when the hard part is not "how do we make a chatbot," but "how do we ship answers that stay tied to our own content."

It works well for:

product help and onboardingdeveloper docsinternal guidesfeature explainerssupport content that already lives in Markdown or MDX
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
What ships today

a setup-focused CLIa local build step that writes .opendetail/index.jsona core Node runtimea thin Next.js adapter for self-hosted routesinstallable client and React hook foundations in the registry
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
Integration modes

| Mode | What you manage | Best for | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| self-hosted | Your route, runtime env, and OPENAI_API_KEY | Private docs, server-owned control, simple default path | Available today |
| hosted | Your built index and client transport config | Faster setup and future hosted delivery | Documented and scaffolded in this phase |
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
Why teams use it

Answers stay tied to your own docsThe runtime stays small and inspectableThe request contract stays narrow: { question: string }The stream contract stays stable across modesMissing documentation fails clearly instead of sounding confident
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text: Start here
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
Human and agent friendly

The docs are organized for people first: task-based quickstarts up front, reference pages after that, and short, direct explanations throughout.

They are also exposed through the Fumadocs machine-readable routes already wired into this app, which means agents can consume the same source material without needing a separate docs format.
chunks.title: Overview

chunks.text:
Quickstart

This is the fastest path to a working self-hosted setup.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
1. Install the package

npm i opendetail
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
2. Scaffold the default setup

bunx opendetail setup --with-media

That command can:

write opendetail.tomlscaffold src/app/api/opendetail/route.tsbuild .opendetail/index.json
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
3. Add your runtime key

Set OPENAI_API_KEY in the app runtime environment before you send requests to the route.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
4. Check the generated config

The default shape looks like this:

version = 1
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/docs"

[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/content-media"
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
5. Rebuild when docs change

bunx opendetail build
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
6. Ask a question

With the default Next.js route in place, post JSON like this:

{ "question": "How do I install this?" }

The route streams NDJSON events in this order:

metasourcesimagesdeltadoneerror
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Hosted instead

If you want the hosted integration shape without generating a route:

bunx opendetail setup --integration hosted

That still writes config and builds the local index, but it skips route scaffolding on purpose.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text: Next steps
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
CLI

OpenDetail ships with a setup-focused CLI.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Commands

opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail setup [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail doctor [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
setup

setup writes the standard project shape for the selected integration mode.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
What it writes

opendetail.tomlin self-hosted, a route at src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts by default.opendetail/index.json unless you pass --skip-build
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Important flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --integration | self-hosted or hosted |
| --config | Custom config path |
| --route | Custom route path for self-hosted setup |
| --base-path | Public source URL base, default /docs |
| --include | Custom docs glob |
| --media-include | Custom media glob |
| --with-media | Include [media] config |
| --skip-build | Skip the initial index build |
| --force | Overwrite existing generated files |
| --interactive | Force the setup wizard |
| --no-interactive | Skip the setup wizard |
| --cwd | Run against another working directory |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
build

build reads opendetail.toml, parses the matched content, and writes the local index artifact.

bunx opendetail build

Optional flags:

--config--output--cwd
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
doctor

doctor checks that the expected files and env are present for the selected integration mode.

For self-hosted, it checks:

config filebuilt indexroute fileOPENAI_API_KEY

For hosted, it checks:

config filebuilt indexOPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT

If any check fails, the command exits with an error.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Common examples

# default self-hosted setup
bunx opendetail setup --with-media

# hosted scaffolding
bunx opendetail setup --integration hosted

# build after editing docs
bunx opendetail build

# diagnose setup issues
bunx opendetail doctor

# hosted diagnostics
bunx opendetail doctor --integration hosted
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Client

The registry transport layer exposes createOpenDetailClient.

Use it when you want the streaming contract without the React hook.
chunks.title: Client
Client

chunks.text:
Options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Request URL, default /api/opendetail |
| headers | Static headers or a function returning headers |
| credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
chunks.title: Client

chunks.text:
Request shape

The client sends:

{ "question": "..." }

with:

accept: application/x-ndjson, application/jsoncontent-type: application/json
chunks.title: Client

chunks.text:
Status values

The client exposes these statuses:

idlependingstreamingerror
chunks.title: Client

chunks.text:
Stream event types

metasourcesimagesdeltadoneerror
chunks.title: Client

chunks.text:
Stop behavior

Calling stop() aborts the active request, clears the internal abort controller, and returns the client to idle.

If you call submit() while another request is active, the previous request is stopped first.
chunks.title: Client

chunks.text:
Configuration

OpenDetail reads configuration from opendetail.toml in the app root.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Minimal config

version = 1
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/docs"
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Fields

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | yes | Must be 1 |
| include | yes | Glob patterns for Markdown and MDX source files |
| exclude | no | Ignore globs for files matched by include |
| base_path | no | URL base used when source URLs are generated |
| media | no | Rules for mapping local media references to public URLs |
| remote_resources | no | OpenAI Responses API retrieval tools to use at answer time |
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Media

Use [media] when your docs reference local images such as ./hero.png.

[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/content-media"

When media is configured:

http:// and https:// URLs are returned as-isroot-relative /... URLs are returned as-islocal relative paths are mapped only if they match [media].includeunsupported absolute schemes and protocol-relative URLs are ignored

If a doc references a local image that does not match the configured media globs, the build fails clearly.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Remote resources

OpenDetail can add Responses API tools to retrieve external sources at answer time.

[remote_resources.file_search]
vector_store_ids = ["vs_123"]
max_num_results = 8

[remote_resources.web_search]
allowed_domains = ["platform.openai.com", "docs.example.com"]
search_context_size = "low"
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
file_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| vector_store_ids | yes | OpenAI vector store IDs |
| max_num_results | no | Limit from 1 to 50 |
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
web_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| allowed_domains | no | Restrict web search to listed domains |
| search_context_size | no | low, medium, or high |

Remote citations are added to the returned sources list with kind: "remote".
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Base path behavior

base_path is normalized before URLs are generated. Use it to match the public route where your docs live, for example /docs or /help.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
React hook

The registry ships a React hook named useOpenDetail for browser-side state and streaming UI.
chunks.title: React hook
React hook
React hook

chunks.text:
Options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Shortcut for transport.endpoint |
| transport.endpoint | Request URL, defaults to /api/opendetail |
| transport.headers | Static headers or a function returning headers |
| transport.credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| transport.fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
| persistence.key | Persist thread state under a storage key |
| persistence.storage | local or session, defaults to local |
chunks.title: React hook

chunks.text:
Returned state

The hook returns:

messagesquestionsetQuestionsubmitstopclearThreadstatustop-level error fields such as error, errorCode, errorRetryable, and errorStatus
chunks.title: React hook

chunks.text:
Message model

The hook stores a thread as user and assistant messages.

Assistant messages track:

streamed textsourcesimagesmodelduration labelerror metadatastatus: pending, streaming, complete, or error
chunks.title: React hook

chunks.text:
Persistence behavior

When persistence is enabled, the hook stores the current thread and input value. If a refresh interrupts a streamed reply, the restored message is marked clearly instead of pretending the reply completed cleanly.
chunks.title: React hook

chunks.text:
Runtime

Use the core runtime when you want to call OpenDetail directly from Node.
chunks.title: Runtime
Runtime

chunks.text:
Result shape

answer() returns:

textmodelsourcesimagesfallback

stream() returns the same metadata plus a ReadableStream<Uint8Array>.
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Assistant instructions

OpenDetail loads project-specific assistant instructions in this order:

assistantInstructions optionassistantInstructionsPath option.opendetail/OPENDETAIL.mdOPENDETAIL.md at the project root

If none exist, only the built-in grounding instructions are used.
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Remote retrieval

The runtime can use remoteResources passed directly into createOpenDetail, or fall back to remote_resources from opendetail.toml.

Those settings create Responses API tools for:

file_searchweb_search
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Defaults

| Setting | Default |
| --- | --- |
| model | gpt-5.4-mini |
| reasoningEffort | none |
| verbosity | low |
| store | false |
| promptCacheRetention | in-memory |
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Important options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| cwd | Working directory used to resolve config and instruction files |
| indexPath | Custom path to the built index |
| indexData | Provide a preloaded index artifact directly |
| client | Reuse an existing OpenAI client |
| assistantInstructions | Inline project guidance |
| assistantInstructionsPath | Custom path for project guidance |
| promptCacheKey | Explicit cache key override |
| remoteResources | Override remote retrieval config |
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Fallback behavior

If no local chunks match the question, OpenDetail still sends the request, but marks the result as fallback: true.

If the model returns no usable text, the fallback text is:

This isn't documented in the configured docs yet.
chunks.title: Runtime
